outlook.headline: Automotive semis inflecting up as destocking ends and ADAS content secular growth reaccelerates
outlook.summary: After a 2025 trough, automotive demand turned stronger-than-expected in 2026Q1, with ADI and Renesas posting record auto results amid restocking and Micron citing improving pricing. Durable secular drivers (L2+/L4 ADAS, rising silicon content per vehicle) underpin the recovery, though TSMC's auto revenue still fell 7% sequentially and TI stayed near-term cautious.

drivers.label: ADAS / autonomy memory & content
drivers.detail: L2+ ADAS scaling fast; L4 needs ~300GB DRAM vs ~16GB today, driving content growth

drivers.label: Inventory normalization / restocking
drivers.detail: Destocking ending; channel rebuild and short-delivery demand lifting orders above expectations

drivers.label: Stronger-than-expected demand
drivers.detail: Record auto quarters and upside vs guide across Europe, Japan and Japanese OEMs

drivers.label: Rising silicon content per vehicle
drivers.detail: More content across BEV/ICE/hybrid powertrains; secular per-car growth seen continuing

drivers.label: Pricing & mature-node auto capacity
drivers.detail: Continued auto pricing improvement; new mature-node fab (ESMC Germany) built for automotive

risks: China automotive still declining quarter-over-quarter, dragging the regional recovery
Recovery partly restocking-driven; demand could fade once channels refill
TI stays cautious, wanting another quarter to confirm auto's trajectory
TSMC auto revenue still down 7% QoQ and only 4% of sales

2330.TW.point: Auto -7% QoQ and just 4% of revenue, but building ESMC Germany mature-node capacity for automotive

ADI.point: Record auto quarter, up 8% sequentially; guides above-seasonal growth, sees 2026 auto growth

MU.point: Record AEBU >$2.7B; ADAS deployment accelerating, pricing improving, robust long-term auto memory growth

TXN.point: Auto up mid-single-digits YoY, flat sequentially; secular content growth continues but stays near-term cautious

6723.T.point: Auto demand stronger than expected with sell-in/sell-through upside; building channel inventory for rising demand

consensus_note: Agreement on secular ADAS/content growth and a 2026Q1 upturn; split on near-term durability, China, and lagging TSMC

excluded.reason: Only match is a financial 'vehicle' to fund chips for LLM players, not automotive
Automotive/autonomous vehicles appear only inside lists of edge/physical-AI devices; no substantive auto commentary
